meta.setting: Fence shadows, road camps, livery yards, and back trails around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with camp life, wanted heat, reputation, horse care, and precarious survival

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush settlement, road movement, mining towns, and precarious work in the region.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California road-town material, commerce, lodging, and county pressure.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Saddle Tramps cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented road-camp note, coffee saying, or borrowed-name warning generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Drift Riders
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing them as rootless riders, odd-job hands, thieves, messengers, witnesses, and temporary allies
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate saddle tramps as precarious frontier survivors, not comic drifters or noble wanderers.
Tie future moves to borrowed names, temporary camp, horse care, rumor trade, soft crimes, hunger, hidden mercy, and sudden loyalty.
Let them move between town, ranch, mine, road camp, tribal edge, and outlaw company without fully belonging anywhere.
Make them useful to player characters as guides, suspects, allies, liabilities, and mirrors of what a rider can become.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should carry loneliness without turning noble. A saddle tramp owns what a horse can bear and what the town forgot to ask.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A saddle-tramp scene should ask what a person will trade for breakfast, a dry blanket, or a name that is not theirs.
must_preserve: Keep rootlessness practical, human, and morally complicated.
The generated page must include image path images/saddle-tramps.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make poverty cute or rootlessness decorative
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about rootless riders, camp pressure, rumor, horse care, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
